channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Huawei Dr Wen Tong: Do 6G right rather than haste
item.description: On 17th October 2024 in Japan, Dr Wen Tong, Huawei Wireless CTO, delivered a speech titled ‘6G needs to delivery new values for the market’ at CEATEC conference.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Buyer’s guide: How to choose between Microsoft 365 and Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft Office — best known for its Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook productivity applications — is how billions of people around the world work and study, whether they do it from home, an office, a classroom, or a combination of those.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cepsa Exploración y Producción finiquita su plan de digitalización 360 avalando la unión entre TI y negocio
item.description: El 30 de marzo del 2022 la cúpula de Cepsa, multinacional energética española, presentaba en sociedad un nuevo plan estratégico con el que marcaba un antes y un después en el negocio.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Japan, UK and Italy to expedite next-generation fighter jet to replace F-2s and Eurofighter Typhoons
item.description: The defense ministers of Japan, the U.K. and Italy have agreed to accelerate the joint development of a next-generation fighter jet.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Right-wing influencers hyped anti-Ukraine videos made by a TV producer also funded by Russian media
item.description: It's a trend that concerns current and former U.S. intelligence officials and disinformation experts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: The Menendez brothers’ house is all over TikTok following the hit Netflix drama
item.description: With the recent release of the Netflix drama Monsters: The Lyle and Erik Menendez Story and the follow-up documentary The Menendez Brothers, true-crime enthusiasts are now turning their attention to the Beverly Hills mansion where it all went down.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Musk kicks off campaign swing for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk kicked off a campaign swing across Pennsylvania Thursday in support of former President Trump, underlining the importance of the battleground state in the rapidly nearing election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google to block election ads after polls close on Election Day
item.description: Google announced it will block ads about the general election on Nov. 5 after the last polls close. “As we’ve done in the past, we will temporarily pause ads related to U.S. elections after the last polls close on November 5,” Google spokesperson Michael Aciman told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX sues California regulator, claiming its launches were blocked because of Elon Musk’s politics
item.description: SpaceX claimed in a lawsuit filed Tuesday that members of the California Coastal Commission violated Musk’s right to free speech and overstepped the agency’s authority after members cited the billionaire’s conspiratorial comments.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step
item.description: Jeff Baur, the product director for logistics IT for the Navy’s PEO-MLB, said ensuring the new platform meets the users’ needs is a top priority. The post Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: Run a business? Then you need to join this Apple service
item.description: Apple just gave smaller enterprises a chance to compete on a more equal basis with larger concerns with three nice improvements to Apple Business Connect: Business Caller ID, brand identity in Mail, and custom business logos for use with Tap to Pay.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: End user empowerment: Shifting the cybersecurity responsibility
item.description: After 21 years of Cybersecurity Awareness Months, maybe it’s time to reconsider the burden we place on end users. The post End user empowerment: Shifting the cybersecurity responsibility first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel, AMD unite in new x86 alliance to tackle AI, other challenges
item.description: Semiconductor rivals Intel and AMD announced the formation of an x86-processor advisory group that will try to address ever-increasing AI workloads, custom chiplets, and advances in 3D packaging and system architectures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Voter beware: Text scam targets you, no matter where you live
item.description: Reports warn of a scam text falsely claiming that recipients aren’t registered to vote. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals what you need to know and five tips to avoid such schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation
